mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in instances where PAYEMS > 100k and UNRATE < 9.5% are negative in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for residential construction, which correlates with higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods of rising HOUST are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening supply-side capacity, leading to inflationary pressures that drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in instances of INDPRO falling > 1.5% in 1 month are positive in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) creates a 'stagflation' environment that depresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of CPIAUCSL > 215 and FEDFUNDS < 0.5% are positive in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real sales (RSAFS) indicates weakening consumer demand, which correlates with lower corporate revenues and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods of declining RSAFS are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in unemployment (UNRATE) signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which reduces consumer confidence and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in instances of UNRATE rising > 0.5% in 1 month are positive in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST) indicates sectoral imbalances that create uncertainty, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of INDPRO > 100.5 and HOUST < 550 are positive in > 40% of cases.
